message:
feat: rolling updates across core, runtime, harness, and extras

Concurrent agent development batch:
- core/input_parser.rs: input parsing refinements
- demo-showcase/widget_gallery.rs: gallery updates
- extras/text_effects.rs: text effect improvements
- harness/flicker_detection.rs, main.rs: harness enhancements
- pty/virtual_terminal.rs: terminal improvements
- runtime/program.rs, render_thread.rs, terminal_writer.rs: runtime updates
- docs/reference/terminal-compatibility.md: documentation update
- Sync beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2zbk.7","title":"Virtualized List: Fenwick/Segment Tree for Variable Heights","description":"Use prefix-sum trees for O(log n) mapping between scroll offset and item index.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Virtualized List: Fenwick/Segment Tree for Variable Heights
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema
- Math spec: define the model, priors/assumptions, and failure modes; include a reproducible notebook-like derivation in comments/docs
- Guarantees: prefer anytime-valid testing (e-process) or distribution-free calibration (conformal) where applicable

 {"id":"bd-2zd","title":"Implement scroll-region optimization for inline mode","description":"PHASE 2.5 - SCROLL REGION OPTIMIZATION

## Background
Scroll regions (DECSTBM) can optimize inline mode by pinning the UI region and letting logs scroll naturally. This is an OPTIMIZATION, not the baseline.

## Technical Requirements

### DECSTBM Usage
- Set scroll region to exclude UI: ESC[1;{log_bottom}r
- Logs scroll within region, UI stays pinned
- Must restore scroll region on exit

### Capability Gating
Only use when:
- caps.scroll_region == true
- NOT in tmux/screen/zellij (mux passthrough issues)
- Terminal known to support correctly

### Implementation

pub struct ScrollRegionPresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    scroll_region_active: bool,
}

impl ScrollRegionPresenter {
    pub fn setup_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if !self.can_use_scroll_region() {
            return;  // Fall back to overlay
        }
        
        let (_, height) = writer.size();
        let log_bottom = height - self.ui_height;
        
        // Set scroll region (excludes UI at bottom)
        write!(writer, "\x1b[1;{}r", log_bottom)?;
        self.scroll_region_active = true;
    }
    
    pub fn restore_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if self.scroll_region_active {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[r")?;  // Reset to full screen
            self.scroll_region_active = false;
        }
    }
    
    fn can_use_scroll_region(&self) -> bool {
        let caps = TerminalCapabilities::detect();
        caps.scroll_region && 
        !caps.in_tmux &&
        !caps.in_screen &&
        !caps.in_zellij
    }
}

### Mux Detection
- TMUX env var presence
- TERM containing "screen"
- ZELLIJ env var presence

### Resize Handling
On terminal resize:
1. Disable scroll region
2. Recalculate dimensions
3. Re-enable if still appropriate

## Tests Required
- [ ] Scroll region setup/teardown
- [ ] Logs scroll within region
- [ ] UI stays pinned at bottom
- [ ] Mux detection works
- [ ] Resize re-anchoring
- [ ] PTY test: scroll region behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DECSTBM used correctly
- [ ] Capability gating works
- [ ] Mux passthrough disabled
- [ ] Fallback to overlay works
- [ ] Clean restore on exit

Strategy selection from capabilities (ScrollRegion for modern terminals, Hybrid for scroll_region without sync_output, OverlayRedraw for mux environments). DECSTBM setup/teardown, resize handling, cleanup guarantees. 12 new tests, all 108 ftui-runtime tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zd","depends_on_id":"bd-fbp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-30g1","title":"Widget State Persistence","description":"## Overview
Enable widgets to save and restore their state across application sessions.

## Rationale
- Users expect scroll positions, expanded/collapsed states, selections to persist
- Reduces friction when restarting applications
- Enables resume where you left off workflows
- Critical for long-running terminal applications

## Core Components
1. **Stateful Trait**: Opt-in trait for widgets with serializable state
2. **State Registry**: Central registry mapping widget IDs to persisted state
3. **Storage Backend**: Pluggable backend (file, memory, custom)
4. **Versioned State**: Schema versioning for backward compatibility
5. **Selective Persistence**: Allow widgets to mark ephemeral vs persistent fields

## Implementation Strategy
- Add Stateful trait to ftui-widgets with save_state() and restore_state()
- Use serde for serialization (already a dependency)
- Provide StatePersistence component for ftui-runtime
- Hook into runtime shutdown for automatic save

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for each widgets state serialization
- Integration tests for save/restore cycle
- Migration tests for state version upgrades
- Crash-recovery tests (partial writes)

## Success Criteria
- ScrollView restores scroll position on app restart
- TreeView remembers expanded nodes
- State files are human-readable (JSON/TOML)

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-30g1.1","title":"Stateful Trait Definition","description":"Opt-in trait for widgets with persistable state.

## Requirements
- Stateful<S> trait with save/restore
- State key generation (unique per widget)
- Serialization via serde
- Version field for migrations

## Proposed API

```rust
use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};

pub trait Stateful: Sized {
    /// The state type that gets persisted
    type State: Serialize + for<'de> Deserialize<'de> + Default;
    
    /// Unique key identifying this widget instance
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey;
    
    /// Extract current state for persistence
    fn save_state(&self) -> Self::State;
    
    /// Restore state from persistence
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: Self::State);
    
    /// State schema version for migrations
    fn state_version() -> u32 { 1 }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct StateKey {
    pub widget_type: &'static str,
    pub instance_id: String,
}

impl StateKey {
    pub fn new(widget_type: &'static str, id: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    
    /// Generate from widget path in tree
    pub fn from_path(path: &[&str]) -> Self;
}

// Wrapper for versioned state
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct VersionedState<S> {
    version: u32,
    data: S,
}
```

## Example Implementation

```rust
impl Stateful for ScrollView {
    type State = ScrollViewState;
    
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey {
        StateKey::new("ScrollView", &self.id)
    }
    
    fn save_state(&self) -> ScrollViewState {
        ScrollViewState {
            scroll_offset: self.offset,
            content_height: self.content_height,
        }
    }
    
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: ScrollViewState) {
        self.offset = state.scroll_offset.min(self.max_offset());
    }
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
struct ScrollViewState {
    scroll_offset: u16,
    content_height: u16,
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/state/stateful.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_state_key_uniqueness: Different widgets have different keys
- test_save_restore_cycle: Round-trip preserves state
- test_default_state: Missing state uses Default
- test_version_mismatch: Handled gracefully
- test_partial_state: Missing fields use defaults

## Widgets to Implement
| Widget | State Fields |
|--------|--------------|
| ScrollView | scroll_offset |
| TreeView | expanded_nodes: HashSet<NodeId> |
| Table | sort_column, sort_dir, filter |
| Tabs | active_index |
| SplitView | split_position |

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Stateful Trait Definition
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile; include isomorphism proof for perf changes
- Unit tests: edge cases + regression fixtures; property tests for invariants where applicable
- E2E: deterministic mode + verbose JSONL logs (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums)

@@ -79,6 +79,18 @@ Notes:
 - Scroll region optimization is optional and disabled in multiplexers.
 - Cursor save/restore is universally supported.
 
+## Quirk Catalog (Simulator)
+
+The PTY simulator (`ftui-pty` `VirtualTerminal`) can apply explicit quirk
+profiles via `QuirkSet` to reproduce known terminal oddities in tests.
+
+- **tmux nested cursor save/restore**: In alt-screen, DEC save/restore (`ESC 7/8`)
+  is ignored to model nested tmux cursor quirks.
+- **GNU screen immediate wrap**: Writing the last column immediately wraps to
+  the next line (cursor moves to column 0 after the write).
+- **Windows console no alt-screen**: DEC 1049/1047 alternate screen sequences
+  are ignored; output stays on the main buffer.
+
 ## Recommended Test Matrix
 
 For CI or manual verification, test against:
